2024-11-18 18:38:21.508679_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: In 1917, after two separate revolutions occurred, the people overthrew the aristocracy and put the Bolsheviks in power. With that, the USSR was born.
2024-11-18 18:38:21.508679_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-18 18:39:21.841817_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During World War One, What caused the political upheaval in Russia?
2024-11-18 18:39:21.841817_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The people of Russia had enough of their Tsars and their lavish lives. They starved and struggled while the Tsars kept making decisions that only impacted them.
2024-11-18 18:39:21.841817_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-18 18:41:01.459734_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Tanks played a role in combat for the first time during WWI. What was the prototype tank called?
2024-11-18 18:41:01.459734_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: In 1915, the UK’s Landship Committee began building a weapon that was intended to turn the tides. Dubbed the Little Willie , after months of designing and manufacturing, the tank made its way to combat in August of that same year.
2024-11-18 18:41:01.459734_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-18 18:44:30.119802_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During World War One, Germany’s decision to attack two fronts was a controversial one. In what document was this plan outlined?
2024-11-18 18:44:30.119802_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Germany intended to attack the French on one side and the Russians on the other. The Schlieffen Plan would lay this plan out in detail.
2024-11-18 18:44:30.119802_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-18 18:46:58.556558_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During World War One, Trench warfare was new, so there was a cost unknown other than the artillery and gunfire. The soldiers in the trenches experienced many medical conditions. Name two different ailments they struggled with.
2024-11-18 18:46:58.556558_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Two medical ailments have been given their name for their extensive proliferation during the Great War. The first and most common was the Trench Foot. This condition was caused by long periods of exposure to dampness, cold, and unsanitary conditions. Many of the men were stuck in trenches, no matter the weather and without access to dry clothes. Because of this, they would end up with trench foot, which could lead to amputation in severe circumstances. The other ailment was known as Trench Mouth. This disease was a severe form of necrotic gingivitis. It was contracted by not having access to hygiene tools and exposure to unsanitary situations like the trenches of WWI.
2024-11-18 18:46:58.556558_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-18 18:48:08.459686_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During World War One, The first battle of the eastern front was the Battle of Tannenberg. Why was it so important other than it is the first battle?
2024-11-18 18:48:08.459686_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: For the Central Powers, this battle showed the weakness of the Russian Army. This battle would lead Germany and its allies to be emboldened and put a little more effort into fighting on the eastern front. (Though most of the focus was still on the western front.)
2024-11-18 18:48:08.459686_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
US History
2024-11-19 09:52:16.325243_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: After America joined World War One, there were a lot of open jobs. Because of this, a lot of African Americans moved to the north to fill these positions. What was this called?
2024-11-19 09:52:16.325243_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Not long removed from the end of the civil war and still under the oppression of Jim Crow laws, the blacks of the south wanted to escape the systematic racism of the south. With the mass opening of jobs due to the war efforts, there was a need for workers, so the Great Migration began.
2024-11-19 09:52:16.325243_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 09:53:53.219751_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Relating to World War One: To have enough soldiers to send into battle, President Wilson and Congress enacted what act?
2024-11-19 09:53:53.219751_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text:
The Selective Service Act

When the U.S. decided to help their allies out, they were not suited for conflict of this size, so they needed a way to get a ton of men into the service. On May 18, 1917, the Selective Service Act was passed. The law would give the president the power to draft non-disabled men into the military.
2024-11-19 09:53:53.219751_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
US History
2024-11-19 09:55:11.744870_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During World War One, To keep the public from panicking and help prevent protests, the U.S. government passed an act that would make it illegal to criticize the government in publications or by individuals. What was the act called?
2024-11-19 09:55:11.744870_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The act was called the Sedition Act.
2024-11-19 09:55:11.744870_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 09:56:29.132877_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: When did the Sedition Act, implemented by the United States, go into effect?
2024-11-19 09:56:29.132877_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The bill was signed into law in April of 1917, just a few weeks after Wilson decreed war.
2024-11-19 09:56:29.132877_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 09:58:16.287459_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: War is a pricey endeavor, and when America joined WW I, they did not have enough to fund the war. So what actions were taken to help with this problem?
2024-11-19 09:58:16.287459_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: To absorb some of the costs, the U.S. government instituted an income tax, war profits tax, and an excise tax. Along with the placement of taxes on this fund, the government also began selling government bonds.
2024-11-19 09:58:16.287459_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 09:58:53.663968_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During World War One, who was the commander of the Austro-Hungarian armies?
2024-11-19 09:58:53.663968_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Franz Conrad von Hötzendorf led the Austro-Hungarian Armies.
2024-11-19 09:58:53.663968_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 09:59:54.055219_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During World War One, What did Charles Whittlesey use to stop the friendly fire from bombarding his troop's position?
2024-11-19 09:59:54.055219_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: During the battle in the Argonne Forest, Whittlesey and his troops (which would later become known as the Lost Battalion) were trapped, surrounded by enemy forces. During the battle, his battalion would begin taking fire from the Allies, and he would use a carrier pigeon to send a message, letting the Allies know that their artillery was falling on comrades.
2024-11-19 09:59:54.055219_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:00:31.680852_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During World War One, How many German soldiers did Alvin York and his unit capture?
2024-11-19 10:00:31.680852_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: During the famous battle of the Meuse-Argonne Offensive on October 8, 1918, Sergeant York and the men of the 82nd Division would capture 132 German soldiers.
2024-11-19 10:00:31.680852_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:01:33.834542_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Relating to WWI, What was the name of the U.S. aviation hero that was known by the nickname “The Balloon Buster”?
2024-11-19 10:01:33.834542_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: In a single week, Frank Luke took down 14 enemy aviators. Ten of those were surveillance balloons aimed at relaying tactical locations and troop movements to the German Army. For this amazing and heroic feat, the pilot was soon dubbed “The Balloon Buster.”
2024-11-19 10:01:33.834542_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:02:38.599591_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During World War One, The German aviation corps had the Red Baron, but what U.S. aviator had the most victories?
2024-11-19 10:02:38.599591_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The most famous American flying ace of WWI was Second Lieutenant Eddie Rickenbacker. In his flying carrier, the pilot shot down 26 enemies, 22 planes, and four balloons.
2024-11-19 10:02:38.599591_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:03:40.590857_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: As of World War One, How old was the youngest soldier to receive a Victoria Cross?
2024-11-19 10:03:40.590857_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: This honor would go to a young man who stuck to his post even after being fatally wounded. First Class Jahn Cromwell would receive this honor at the very young age of 16.
2024-11-19 10:03:40.590857_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:04:18.818590_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: How many Victoria Crosses were awarded during and for actions taken in World War I?
2024-11-19 10:04:18.818590_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Roughly 634 were awarded for bravery and acts of heroism in the face of extreme danger.
2024-11-19 10:04:18.818590_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:05:06.686426_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Everyone knows about the Red Baron, but there was another famous German flying ace that was a hero to his people. What was his name, and how many enemy planes did he shoot down?
2024-11-19 10:05:06.686426_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The runner-up in the German aviation corps was Ernst Udet. This pilot would work throughout the war and shoot down a total of 61 enemy planes.
2024-11-19 10:05:06.686426_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:05:52.330704_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Just a year after WWI began, Russia’s ruler took control of the reigns of the army. When did he do this?
2024-11-19 10:05:52.330704_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: To reinvigorate his army, Tsar Nicholas II would take complete control over the Russian military on September 5, 1915.
2024-11-19 10:05:52.330704_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:06:28.745461_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, To make sure that the BEF had enough recruits to fight the war, the government instituted conscription. When did this go into effect?
2024-11-19 10:06:28.745461_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The British government activated the conscription ruling on January 27, 1916.
2024-11-19 10:06:28.745461_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:12:00.237345_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Getting your soldiers and weapons behind enemy objects is always a play when in the midst of war. During WW I, Germans were able to get a U-Boat in U.S. waters during the war. What U-boat was this and when did it arrive in U.S. waters?
2024-11-19 10:12:00.237345_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: This was the first enemy vessel in U.S. waters since 1812. The German U-boat, U-151 , entered the waters on the east coast with the mission of laying mines and cutting underwater telegraph lines. The submarine entered these waters on May 25, 1918.
2024-11-19 10:12:00.237345_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
military science
2024-11-19 10:14:11.720211_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: After the First Battle of Marne, WW I would be fought in the trenches, but that wasn’t the only warfare that this conflict saw. There were two military tactics used that had never been used before: what were they?
2024-11-19 10:14:11.720211_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text:
Airplanes, radio intercepts, and automotive transport of troops

Part of the reason the conflict at Marne was so successful was due to air surveillance, but this would not have been so effective without the radio intercepts. Airplanes were relatively new to combat, and being able to receive an audio transmission on troop movements leveled the playing field a bit a more. Along with radio intercepts, the use of the “Marne taxis” was the first use of the automotive transport of troops. Before this, it would have taken days for the soldiers to arrive to replenish the French Army. Going forward, these two military tactics would become the norm.
2024-11-19 10:14:11.720211_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:14:43.439877_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, What advantages did the British fleet have over the German Navy?
2024-11-19 10:14:43.439877_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The most significant advantage was the number of ships. In comparison to the German fleet, the British had 37 heavy vessels versus the Germans’ 27 boats. For the smaller vessels, the British had 113 versus the Germans’ 72. On top of the advantage of having more ships, they also cracked the German signal codes early, which allowed them to maneuver to counteract the Germans’ tactical movements and brace for any potential attacks.
2024-11-19 10:14:43.439877_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:19:46.712426_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WWI, How did Vice Admiral Beatty lose three ships in the initial skirmishes of the Battle of Jutland?
2024-11-19 10:19:46.712426_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: These three battleships were not equipped with anti-flash protection. This flaw in their design meant that when shelled by the German fleet, the fire was able to make it into the powder houses. In turn, this caused massive explosions, damaging the ships enough that they would end up sinking or very damaged that they were useless to the naval efforts.
2024-11-19 10:19:46.712426_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:20:44.054454_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, What time did the second phase of the Battle of Jutland start?
2024-11-19 10:20:44.054454_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: At 7:15 on the evening of May 31, the German fleet ran into the Grand Fleet.
2024-11-19 10:20:44.054454_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:21:37.248045_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, What was the opening maneuver of the Battle of Jutland?
2024-11-19 10:21:37.248045_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: To form a blockade, the British ships wheeled to port 90 degrees. This maneuver prevented the German fleet from reaching their home port to recoup their losses.
2024-11-19 10:21:37.248045_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:22:35.312068_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, How did the German fleet escape the Battle of Jutland?
2024-11-19 10:22:35.312068_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Admiral Scheer, knowing the battle and the fleet may well be lost if he couldn’t retreat, had his ships do three separate 180-degree turns. This maneuver allowed the fleet to elude the tactics of their enemy and find a way to get back to Wilhelmshaven to recoup. In this battle, both sides took significant damage.
2024-11-19 10:22:35.312068_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:23:34.487444_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, How many people and tons were lost in the Battle of Jutland?
2024-11-19 10:23:34.487444_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The British lost approximately 6,784 men and 111,000 tons. In comparison, the Germans lost 3,050 men and 62,000 tons.
2024-11-19 10:23:34.487444_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:24:06.669377_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, How did the Battle of Jutland change the German naval strategy during WWI?
2024-11-19 10:24:06.669377_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The superiority of the British fleet was evident, so they course-corrected and began an attack on the British economy. This decision meant using their ships and U-Boats to attack commercial vessels.
2024-11-19 10:24:06.669377_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:24:48.265613_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, Why did the German military strategists feel that Verdun was an excellent place to execute this attack?
2024-11-19 10:24:48.265613_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The Germans were looking for a bastion of French pride, one that would drive the French to throw their soldiers onto the battle, no matter the cost. It also didn’t hurt that the citadel was threatening the efficiency of the German communications line.
2024-11-19 10:24:48.265613_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:25:21.667236_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, Who commanded a vital army division in the field and on the ground for the German forces?
2024-11-19 10:25:21.667236_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Though the chief commander was General Erich von Falkenhayn, the ground soldiers of the Fifth Army were led by Crown Prince Wilhelm, who was the current Kaiser’s eldest son.
2024-11-19 10:25:21.667236_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:25:59.716287_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, How did the French come to be alerted to the German movement in the Verdun region?
2024-11-19 10:25:59.716287_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: In January, French reconnaissance planes noticed a strange surge of German troops along the area. However, it wasn’t until February that French intelligence reported back to command of the forces amassing on the right bank of the Meuse River.
2024-11-19 10:25:59.716287_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:26:32.028187_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, How long was the motorized supply train, and how many vehicles were in the convoy executed by the French for the Battle of Verdun?
2024-11-19 10:26:32.028187_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The motorized supply train used for the first time in warfare to help the French stay supplied with both equipment and men drove down a 37-mile stretch of road. On this dirt road, about 3,000 trucks would travel, loaded down with the essentials to keep the front lines replenished and reinforced.
2024-11-19 10:26:32.028187_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:27:45.624311_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, the road used to supply french troops to the Battle of Verdun was called what?
2024-11-19 10:27:45.624311_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The road would come to be known as the La Voie Sacrée or the Sacred Way.
2024-11-19 10:27:45.624311_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:28:34.677074_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, Who was the leader of the French forces at Verdun?
2024-11-19 10:28:34.677074_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: At the beginning of the Battle of Verdun, the French High Command felt that there could be no better leader than the “Victor of Marne,” General Joseph Jacques Césaire Joffre.
2024-11-19 10:28:34.677074_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:29:51.773460_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, at the Battle of Verdun, who replaced General Joseph Jacques Césaire Joffre? and Why?
2024-11-19 10:29:51.773460_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: In May, after several defeats, the high command had lost faith in Joffre and decided to replace him with General Philippe Pétain.
2024-11-19 10:29:51.773460_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:30:33.113476_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, How many shells did the Germans unleash at the initial artillery strike of the Battle of Verdun, and how long did it last?
2024-11-19 10:30:33.113476_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The opening salvo of the Battle of Verdun took eight hours, and the German artillery launched approximately two million shells onto the French defensive line.
2024-11-19 10:30:33.113476_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:31:16.723412_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, How many divisions did the German and Allies have at their disposal for the German Spring Offensive of 1918?
2024-11-19 10:31:16.723412_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: In preparation for the Spring Offensive, the Germans upped their presence on the western front. That left the German Army with 191 divisions available for combat during the Spring Offensive. For the Allies, they only had access to 178 divisions.
2024-11-19 10:31:16.723412_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:31:46.437373_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, What was the code name given by Ludendorff to the first wave of the Spring Offensive?
2024-11-19 10:31:46.437373_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The German code name for the opening skirmish of the Spring Offensive was Michael.
2024-11-19 10:31:46.437373_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:32:41.225712_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, the opening salvo of the Spring Offensive was executed when?
2024-11-19 10:32:41.225712_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The Spring Offensive would launch on March 21, 1918, and would do so with a five-hour bombardment of the French defensive line.
2024-11-19 10:32:41.225712_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:47:22.716193_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, How many mortars were used in the opening salvo of the Spring Offensive?
2024-11-19 10:47:22.716193_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: During this battle, 6,473 bullets would be fired as well as 3,532 mortars were lobed into that defensive line.
2024-11-19 10:47:22.716193_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:48:06.239306_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, What two factors gave the Allies an advantage at the Battle of Amiens?
2024-11-19 10:48:06.239306_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The advantages that made a huge difference were all types of deception. The first came backed by bogus communication. The Allies moved troops from the front lines to make it seem as though defenses were waning. In truth, the armies had been reinforced with nightly troop movements by other members of the Allied coalition. The Allies also used smoke screens as they began their push into German territory. The smoke gave them a tactical advantage over the weakened and demoralized German Army.
2024-11-19 10:48:06.239306_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:48:49.386338_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, What hampered the success of the Gallipoli Campaign?
2024-11-19 10:48:49.386338_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: There was one main problem that kept the British forces from being successful in executing the Gallipoli Campaign. The first was a lack of intelligence on the people or the terrain. This lack of understanding of the ground, as well as the level of Turkish resistance, helped keep the British and their allies perpetually off-kilter as they went about executing their plans.
2024-11-19 10:48:49.386338_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
geography
2024-11-19 10:50:16.752450_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: What two bodies of water connect via the Dardanelles?
2024-11-19 10:50:16.752450_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The Dardanelles connected the Aegean Sea with the Marmara Sea.
2024-11-19 10:50:16.752450_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
geography
2024-11-19 10:51:09.675526_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Why was the Dardanelles straight important to the WW I Effort?
2024-11-19 10:51:09.675526_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: By taking this strait, it would have allowed the Allied Forces to safely transport supplies and troops through the strait to reinforce the Russian forces. This would help the Russian troops to stave off the advances of the Ottoman Empire.
2024-11-19 10:51:09.675526_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:51:39.704381_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, Who was one of the British leaders who led the naval attack on the Dardanelles?
2024-11-19 10:51:39.704381_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: There were several leaders during this battle, but one of the top guys was First Lord of the British Admiralty, Winston Churchill. Churchill would bear the brunt of the fault for the unsuccessful campaign and would be demoted, eventually resigning altogether, and joining the front lines as a soldier. He, of course, would go on to become one of the most important men in the world and the leader of his nation during the next Great War.
2024-11-19 10:51:39.704381_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:52:13.604510_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, After the first wave of attack, the naval battle was unsuccessful at Gallipoli; what happened?
2024-11-19 10:52:13.604510_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: After the initial failed naval attack, the British regrouped and planned a land attack instead that would be backed by naval vessels.
2024-11-19 10:52:13.604510_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:53:01.321191_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, How many casualties were there on both sides during the Gallipoli Campaign?
2024-11-19 10:53:01.321191_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Altogether, the casualties would tally about 500,000. Both sides experienced about 250,000 losses. Of those 250,000 casualties for the Allies, there were approximately 46,000 who lost their lives. On the Ottoman side, they experienced roughly 250,000 losses, of which 65,000 were deaths.
2024-11-19 10:53:01.321191_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:53:42.378383_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, The Turkish resistance forces were led by a man that would later become a Turkish hero. What name does most of the world know Mustafa Kemal?
2024-11-19 10:53:42.378383_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Mustafa Kemal was an imposing figure and one that had a deep sense of pride in his country. He would later go on to lead the way for the Turkish Republic after the Ottoman Empire was dissolved and would come to be known by the name Atatürk.
2024-11-19 10:53:42.378383_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:54:21.019474_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, What happened to Churchill after the loss at Gallipoli?
2024-11-19 10:54:21.019474_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: He was initially demoted to an obscure cabinet position. Feeling slighted and a little by the fact that he was the scapegoat, he resigned and fought on the front lines with the Royal Scot Fusiliers. He would return to politics in 1917, and become the Minister of Munitions, and the rest of his career is, as they say, history.
2024-11-19 10:54:21.019474_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:55:02.261507_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, There were a lot of transitions after the Battle for the Gallipoli Peninsula failed, even the British Prime Minister. Who took over after H.H. Asquith resigned his post?
2024-11-19 10:55:02.261507_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: A liberal government took hold after Asquith resigned and was led by David Lloyd George.
2024-11-19 10:55:02.261507_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:55:37.388589_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, What was the Turkish name of the Gallipoli Campaign?
2024-11-19 10:55:37.388589_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The Turkish call the campaign the Battle of Çanakkale.
2024-11-19 10:55:37.388589_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:56:42.285530_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: What was one of the reasons that the Ottoman Empire joined WW I?
2024-11-19 10:56:42.285530_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Besides the fact they had an alliance with Germany, the Ottomans also thought that when the Central Powers won, they would regain control over the land they had previously ruled over. Places like Egypt and the Balkans were top of their list.
2024-11-19 10:56:42.285530_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:57:25.912871_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Relating to WW I, What two battles made up the Battle of Megiddo?
2024-11-19 10:57:25.912871_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The two battles were the Battle of Sharon and the Battle of Nablus.
2024-11-19 10:57:25.912871_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:58:33.894857_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, the Battle of Sharon took place when?
2024-11-19 10:58:33.894857_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: On September 19, 1918, the initial battle of what would come to be called the Battle of Megiddo started.
2024-11-19 10:58:33.894857_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:59:09.342142_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: When was Baron Richthofen (the Red Baron) born, and where?
2024-11-19 10:59:09.342142_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Baron Manfred von Richthofen, the man known as the Red Baron, was born May 2, 1892, in Prussia—to be exact, what is now known as Breslau, Germany.
2024-11-19 10:59:09.342142_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 10:59:44.372838_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Baron Richthofen was born into a high-class family, and military service was expected. How old was Richthofen when he enrolled in military school?
2024-11-19 10:59:44.372838_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Young Richthofen spent his early youth hunting and playing sports, but when he turned 11, that all came to a stop as he began his career path to military success.
2024-11-19 10:59:44.372838_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:00:08.932999_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: When was the “Red Baron’s” first victory logged?
2024-11-19 11:00:08.932999_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The flying ace's first victory was on September 17, 1916.
2024-11-19 11:00:08.932999_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:00:47.596336_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: The Red Baron's first success, Where was it, and who did he shoot down?
2024-11-19 11:00:47.596336_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: It was over France. There was a battle; he was able to shoot down a British fighter.
2024-11-19 11:00:47.596336_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:01:23.795760_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: What medal did Richthofen earn when he collected his first 16 victories in WW I?
2024-11-19 11:01:23.795760_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The medal was known as the Blue Max by the public, but its official name was the Pour le Mérite.
2024-11-19 11:01:23.795760_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:02:14.424586_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, What was the name of the squadron that Richthofen was given command of?
2024-11-19 11:02:14.424586_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Officially, the name of the unit was Jasta 11. However, because the pilots of this squadron painted their planes in bright colors and were able to do crazy aerial maneuvers, they became known as “The Flying Circus.”
2024-11-19 11:02:14.424586_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:02:41.321423_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, How many men did Cavell help get back to the safety of Allied territory?
2024-11-19 11:02:41.321423_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The numbers are not definitive, but most reports say that she helped approximately 200 soldiers before being captured by the Germans.
2024-11-19 11:02:41.321423_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:03:48.965549_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Relating to WW I, When was Cavell arrested?
2024-11-19 11:03:48.965549_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The Germans had kept a close eye on Cavell with spies and consistent inspections of her facility. Eventually, her extracurricular activities were detected, and in August of 1915, she was arrested and held for trial.
2024-11-19 11:03:48.965549_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:04:48.567455_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, Cavell was arrested, when was she court-martialed?
2024-11-19 11:04:48.567455_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: She was arrested in August, but it wasn’t until October 2 that she was court-martialed, and just two days later, she confessed with pride that she had helped those men escape.
2024-11-19 11:04:48.567455_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:06:41.594970_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Relating to WW I: When was Cavell executed, and how?
2024-11-19 11:06:41.594970_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: She was found guilty of treason and was executed by firing squad on October 12, 1915.
2024-11-19 11:06:41.594970_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:08:46.125187_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Relating to WW I, Who was the first recipient of the Medal of Honor for heroism as a POW?
2024-11-19 11:08:46.125187_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The first recipient was an executive officer from a naval vessel that was captured after his ship sank. Edouard Izac was captured and moved from two different POW camps while keeping the information he had learned on the submarine on his way to the POW camp to himself in the hope the information could help the Allies.
2024-11-19 11:08:46.125187_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:09:29.678007_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, Edouard Izac had made several trans-Atlantic transport runs before a German U-boat sank his ship. How many trips had he made?
2024-11-19 11:09:29.678007_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Izac had made four other voyages delivering supplies and troops to the western front.
2024-11-19 11:09:29.678007_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:10:41.712065_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WWI, Izac escaped from the POW camp, How did he do it?
2024-11-19 11:10:41.712065_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: He waited for the right time, and under cover of darkness, he climbed a barb-wired fence and escaped into the forest surrounding the camp. He worked his way to the Rhine, surviving on raw fruits and vegetables. Once he arrived at the Rhine, he swam across to Switzerland and made his way to the Bureau of Navigation, which he reached on November 11, 1918, the day the war ended.
2024-11-19 11:10:41.712065_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:11:38.388049_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, What other accomplishments (aside from escaping the POW camp) did Izac go on to achieve?
2024-11-19 11:11:38.388049_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Izac would end up representing California in the House of Representatives for California from 1937-1947. He was also a member of the House of Naval Affairs Commission. He was part of this when it inspected the concentration camps in 1945.
2024-11-19 11:11:38.388049_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:13:09.782012_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, What were the 14 points that U.S. President Woodrow Wilson laid out to Congress?
2024-11-19 11:13:09.782012_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text:
When Woodrow Wilson stood in front of the Congress, his plan was to lay out a vision of what the post-war global community would be like. His 14 points included no secret treaties and alliances. He felt that politics should be transparent. The speech also stated that the seas should be neutral territory as well as all countries should be able to have free trade. It called for a global reduction of arms by every nation. The points also stated that Belgium should be reinstituted as neutral and independent once again.

Along with the territorial ideas, he also said that the Alsace-Lorraine should be retired to France and that Italy’s borders should be drawn. Continuing with the boundaries and territorial questions, he also felt that the Balkan nations should be given independence and that those that were still under Turkish rule should be granted freedom. He also thought that the Kingdom of Poland should be given country status and more land. Lastly, these 14 points were the foundations for the League of Nations.
2024-11-19 11:13:09.782012_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:13:55.452917_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Relating to WW I, Who were the big four at the Paris Peace Conference?
2024-11-19 11:13:55.452917_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The big four were the leaders of the big four nations in charge of overseeing the peace conference. This included: Wilson from the U.S., George from Britain, Clemenceau from France, and Orlando from Italy.
2024-11-19 11:13:55.452917_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:14:34.988879_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: WW I wasn’t exactly a world affair. What country was neutral during the war?
2024-11-19 11:14:34.988879_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Norway was the only European country that remained neutral for the war. They even tried to do the same in the next world war, but the Third Reich had different plans and invaded them in 1940.
2024-11-19 11:14:34.988879_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:15:45.909331_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text:
There were a lot of new things to warfare during WW I. Other than trench warfare, what two other military weapons also saw their first action during the conflict?
(Excluding aircraft and mechanized transport)
2024-11-19 11:15:45.909331_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The two pieces of equipment that would wreak havoc on the battlefields of World War I were the tank and the flame thrower. The British introduced the tank at the Battle of Somme. The flame thrower was a weapon produced by the Germans.
2024-11-19 11:15:45.909331_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:16:47.159331_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Over nine million soldiers died during WW I. A good majority of these were under 30. Some were even younger. How old was the youngest soldier to see action?
2024-11-19 11:16:47.159331_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text:
12,

Many young men rushed to their enlistment stations and lied about their ages to join. The youngest was a 12-year-old British boy. Sidney Lewis would put boots to ground for the first time at the Battle of Somme in 1916. Eventually, his mom would produce his birth certificate, and he would be recalled. He would later re-enlist in 1918 and spend time in Austria as part of the army of occupation.
2024-11-19 11:16:47.159331_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:17:21.119976_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Trench warfare was new to WW I and used very effectively on both sides of the western front. Because it worked so well, quite a lot of trenches were dug. How many miles of trenches were there by the end of the war?
2024-11-19 11:17:21.119976_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Throughout the war, the soldiers of both the Central Powers and the Allied Forces dug 15,659 trenches. (That was almost 16,000 miles of trenches along the western front.)
2024-11-19 11:17:21.119976_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
military science
2024-11-19 11:18:14.152040_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, One of the most critical military strategies apart from trench warfare was maneuver warfare. What is the definition of maneuver warfare?
2024-11-19 11:18:14.152040_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: This battle strategy was a very effective form of tactic and resulted in many victories. The basic principle of this strategy is tactics infuse the enemy through disrupting their maneuvers with rapid movement. These swift attacks and actions kept the enemy on their toes, and this increases the chance that they make a mistake.
2024-11-19 11:18:14.152040_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
military science
2024-11-19 11:19:12.560561_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Another form of warfare strategy was known as attrition warfare. What does this strategy entail?
2024-11-19 11:19:12.560561_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: This type of warfare entails wearing down the enemy through the use of tactics like blockades as well as constant bombardment. The intention, when using this style of combat, is to wear your opponent down both physically and emotionally until their will is broken and they can no longer fight. During WWI, this type of warfare happened between 1915-1917.
2024-11-19 11:19:12.560561_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:19:52.053215_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: You have to have the support of the people, and the Prussian Prime Minister knew this. During WW I, What did Otto von Bismarck use to convince German people in the south to support the war efforts?
2024-11-19 11:19:52.053215_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Just 40 years before the outbreak of WWI, the Prussian empire locked horns with France. This is the war that brought a unified Germany and granted Germany control over the Alsace Lorraine. Using a new German pride and the threat of them losing land, he was able to garner support.
2024-11-19 11:19:52.053215_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:20:36.449453_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: The Treaty of Versailles was vital in the revolution of WW I, but one country rejected the treaty. Which country was this?
2024-11-19 11:20:36.449453_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The United States opted to reject the treaty on the grounds that they wanted to stay out of European political affairs.
2024-11-19 11:20:36.449453_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:21:41.863628_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Though WW I ended for everyone with the Treaty of Versailles, the Russians left the battle well before the armistice, and the famous treaty. What was the treaty that allowed Russia to end its war with Germany?
2024-11-19 11:21:41.863628_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Lenin’s decision to pull out of the war came to fruition with the signing of the Treaty of Brest-Litovsk on March 3, 1918. The name of the treaty came from the city it was signed in, which is what we know today as Belarus. With the signing of this treaty, Russia ceded to giving up over a million square miles of land. Germany and the Austro-Hungarian Empire would gain control over Lithuania, Poland, Latvia, and Estonia. The Ottoman Empire would gain Kars, Ardahan, and Batum. Whereas Ukraine, Georgia, and Finland would gain independence. Of course, when Germany lost, they were forced to give up the land they had accrued with this treaty.
2024-11-19 11:21:41.863628_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:22:10.823172_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, The Second Battle of Ypres happened in 1915 in Belgium. The battle lasted a little over a month and was the site of two significant firsts. What were they?
2024-11-19 11:22:10.823172_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The Second Battle of Ypres would see the first use of Canadian troops as well as the first use of gas by the Germans. The Germans would effectively use chlorine gas as a weapon to help them win the battle. The gas would be a vital tool for the Germans, and immediately after this attack, the Allied Forces began working on their gas. The Canadian First Division served at Ypres and lost significant casualties. This battle would also give a brave Canadian the honor of being the first member of the Canadian Army to win a Victoria Cross.
2024-11-19 11:22:10.823172_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:23:39.932646_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Airplanes were new in WW I and needed a few adjustments to make them useful. One of these improvements was a unique propeller. What was this propeller called?
2024-11-19 11:23:39.932646_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The propeller was called the “Systeme Morane.” This propeller was designed with triangular wedges made out of steel and included to stop bullets from striking the propeller.
2024-11-19 11:23:39.932646_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:25:19.872463_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: How big was the eastern front in WW I?
2024-11-19 11:25:19.872463_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The eastern front encompassed 310 miles of land and stretched from Memel (which was in East Prussia but is now part of Lithuania) on the Baltic Sea to Czernowitz on the Romanian border.
2024-11-19 11:25:19.872463_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:26:28.164402_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: How large was the Russian Army when WW I began?
2024-11-19 11:26:28.164402_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: At the beginning of the conflict, the Russian Army held steady at one million soldiers. By the end of the war, the army had risen to over three million.
2024-11-19 11:26:28.164402_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:27:26.220456_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, The Germans had the Schlieffen Plan, what was the Russian plan of attack called?
2024-11-19 11:27:26.220456_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text:
Schedule 19:
The Russians developed a plan of attack that would have them splitting their forces. In the north, the Russian Army would take two of its divisions and attack east Prussia, while three armies would strike Austria-Hungary. This plan was called Schedule 19.
2024-11-19 11:27:26.220456_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:28:57.603217_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: The Lusitania may be the most famous passenger ship sunk by German U-boats, but it was not the first. What was the first passenger ship to be sunk?
2024-11-19 11:28:57.603217_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: On March 11, 1915, the German U-boat, U-28 , sunk the British passenger ship RMS Falaba . The ship left Liverpool bound for the western African coast. Off the smalls of the St. George Channel, the German U-boat attacked and fired on the boat.
2024-11-19 11:28:57.603217_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
US History
2024-11-19 11:30:03.586663_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: WW I spread across the globe, even onto U.S. soil. A group of German sympathizers attacked a factory, blowing it up in Jersey City. What did the factory make?
2024-11-19 11:30:03.586663_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: To help their comrades across the Atlantic, a group of German sympathizers snuck in and planted bombs in the Black Tom Island Munitions Plant on July 30, 1916. This plant was making weapons and ammunition for U.S. troops that had just recently entered the war effort. They hoped to stop the flow of supplies to the Allied Forces. In the explosion, four people were confirmed dead, but that number may be up to seven.
2024-11-19 11:30:03.586663_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:30:35.606972_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: WW I ended with an armistice, not an actual surrender. There were two separate treaties, one for each front. When did each central power sign their armistices?
2024-11-19 11:30:35.606972_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: After the fall at Trieste, the Austro-Hungarian Empire signed the Armistice of Villa Giusti. This treaty was signed on November 3, 1918. On the western front, the armistice was signed on November 11, after the Battle of Redonthes at Le Francport.
2024-11-19 11:30:35.606972_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:31:33.378550_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: What is another name for the Battle of Jutland, which occurred during WW I?
2024-11-19 11:31:33.378550_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The major naval battle is also known as the Battle of Skagerrak.
2024-11-19 11:31:33.378550_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:32:07.930458_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, Who was the chief of the general staff and principal strategist for the German forces at Verdun?
2024-11-19 11:32:07.930458_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Erich von Falkenhayn was a Prussian general of some note before he took charge of the forces at Verdun. He gained experience training the Chinese soldiers that would fight against the boxers in the rebellion. He was promoted to the Prussian Minister of War before replacing General Helmuth von Moltke as the chief of the general of staff in 1914.
2024-11-19 11:32:07.930458_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:33:04.178674_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, What was Falkenhayn’s edict for the Battle of Verdun?
2024-11-19 11:33:04.178674_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Falkenhayn went into the Battle of Verdun with strategy and goals. These goals seemed extreme to some as he wanted a five-to-one kill ratio and to take the citadel at Verdun at all costs. His was a war of attrition, and he didn’t care how much French or German blood was needed to be spilled to achieve his ultimate goal.
2024-11-19 11:33:04.178674_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:33:35.564178_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, What happened to Falkenhayn after the failure of the Verdun Offensive?
2024-11-19 11:33:35.564178_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The failure of the Verdun campaign haunted Falkenhayn for the rest of his life. After the significant defeat and loss of soldiers, the German High Command decided he needed to be dismissed.
2024-11-19 11:33:35.564178_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:34:33.403143_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Where did (and still do) the bones of the fallen soldiers end up after the Battle of Verdun?
2024-11-19 11:34:33.403143_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: When the battle was over, the dead found a home at Douaumont Ossuary. To this day, bones discovered are taken to the ossuary and laid to rest with the rest of their comrades.
2024-11-19 11:34:33.403143_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:35:42.049151_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WWI, After the initial offensive, the Spring Offensive, how much land was covered?
2024-11-19 11:35:42.049151_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: After the first battle of the Spring Offensive, the Germans had gained 1,197 square miles of land and had taken 90,000 prisoners.
2024-11-19 11:35:42.049151_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:36:14.293765_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, What was the second wave during the Spring Offensive’s code name?
2024-11-19 11:36:14.293765_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The second wave of Spring Offensive was executed on April 9, 1918, and was less successful than the initial foray. This troop movement was dubbed Operation Georgette.
2024-11-19 11:36:14.293765_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:36:45.812109_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, What did the Battle of Amiens and the subsequent battles become known as?
2024-11-19 11:36:45.812109_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The Battle of Amiens was the opening battle of what would later become called the “Hundred Days” Offensive. This push would be the final nail in the German Army’s coffin and lead to the armistice.
2024-11-19 11:36:45.812109_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:37:09.870076_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, How many lines of trenches did the Germans have at the Battle of Amiens?
2024-11-19 11:37:09.870076_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The German defensive line was fortified by a sequence of three lines of trenches. These trenches, however, were poorly crafted with inefficiently placed wire along with the top and poorly-built dugouts.
2024-11-19 11:37:09.870076_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:38:56.195747_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, there was a gun called the "Amiens gun", what was it?
2024-11-19 11:38:56.195747_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The “Amiens Gun” was a German 280mm Krupp naval gun mounted to a railway carriage.
2024-11-19 11:38:56.195747_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:39:29.082200_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, How many infantry divisions were in Rawlinson’s Fourth Army?
2024-11-19 11:39:29.082200_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: Once Rawlinson’s Fourth Army was beefed up, there was a total of 14 different infantry divisions along with several cavalry and tank units.
2024-11-19 11:39:29.082200_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:40:04.465020_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, How many pieces of artillery, tanks, and aircraft played a part in the Battle of Amiens on the side of the Allied Forces?
2024-11-19 11:40:04.465020_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: On the Allied side, the Battle of Amiens was stacked. Not only did they have 14 infantry divisions but also a total of over 2,000 artillery guns, over 600 tanks, and 1,900 plus several aircraft.
2024-11-19 11:40:04.465020_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:40:37.088734_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, How many divisions, guns, and aircraft did the German Second Army have at the Battle of Amiens?
2024-11-19 11:40:37.088734_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: In the corner of the German defenses, the Second Army was stocked with ten divisions, 530 guns, and 365 planes.
2024-11-19 11:40:37.088734_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:42:12.244365_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: Relating to WW I, What was a “Whippet” tank?
2024-11-19 11:42:12.244365_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: The Allied Forces had a tank dubbed the “Whippet” tank. This tank was a lighter, faster tank that the Allies used for scouting missions.
2024-11-19 11:42:12.244365_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d

subject: history
2024-11-19 11:42:45.696605_47d39d7b-37ff.question_text: During WW I, What Russian Army was annihilated at the Battle of Tannenberg?
2024-11-19 11:42:45.696605_47d39d7b-37ff.answer_text: As the battle came to a close, the Second Army was led by Aleksander Samsonov. With no help from his fellow generals, Samsonov’s men were outmaneuvered and found themselves running to the cover of the forests, but it was too late; the defeat was imminent.
2024-11-19 11:42:45.696605_47d39d7b-37ff.author: 47d39d7b-37ff-461b-aeec-ca52e36c101d
